etymology.description: semantic 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") + phonetic 曷(曷) -> expression involving the mouth -> to drink
etymology.interpretation: The semantic element 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") indicates an action related to the mouth, such as drinking, while the phonetic component 曷(曷) provides the sound. This combination naturally leads to the meaning of 'drink', as drinking involves using the mouth.

examples.chinese: 我每天早上都喝一杯咖啡(我每天早上都喝一杯咖啡)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ měitiān zǎoshang dōu hē yī bēi kāfēi.
examples.english: I drink a cup of coffee every morning.

examples.chinese: 她喜欢在夏天喝冰茶(她喜歡在夏天喝冰茶)。
examples.pinyin: Tā xǐhuān zài xiàtiān hē bīngchá.
examples.english: She likes to drink iced tea in the summer.

examples.chinese: 小明喝完了整瓶水(小明喝完了整瓶水)。
examples.pinyin: Xiǎomíng hē wán le zhěng píng shuǐ.
examples.english: Xiaoming drank the entire bottle of water.
